title: Requests, Limits, QoS, Probes, Startup, Readiness, and Resource Failure · API Object Lens: K8s Probe Design Budgets
summary: A slow-starting API is killed by liveness checks, serves traffic before warmup, and competes for memory without meaningful requests or limits. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s probe design budgets increment in health and resource behavior policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design HTTP, TCP, gRPC, or exec probes with correct target, timeout, period, thresholds, resource cost, failure budget, and diagnostic output.
Separate startup gating, traffic readiness, process liveness, container restart, endpoint conditions, and dependency health.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Probe Design Budgets · Theory K8s Probe Design Budgets
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory service passes liveness while serving stale data, has no startup probe, requests 100m but uses 500m CPU, and is OOMKilled under pressure. Redesign probes, requests, limits, QoS, graceful termination, and changed-load checks.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the K8s Probe Design Budgets contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example service passes liveness while serving stale data, has no startup probe, requests 100m but uses 500m CPU, and is OOMKilled under pressure. Redesign probes, requests, limits, QoS, graceful termination, and changed-load checks. Competency probe: Design HTTP, TCP, gRPC, or exec probes with correct target, timeout, period, thresholds, resource cost, failure budget, and diagnostic output. A defensible solution applies K8s Probe Design Budgets, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to health and resource behavior policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-probe-design-budgets — Design HTTP, TCP, gRPC, or exec probes with correct target, timeout, period, thresholds, resource cost, failure budget, and diagnostic output.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-health-resources-qos-3
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-health-resources-qos-3
    artifact: health-resource-policy.yaml
    decision: worked k8s health resources qos 3
    environment: shared multi-tenant cluster under Restricted Pod Security
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      requests: {cpu: 200m, memory: 256Mi}
      limits: {memory: 512Mi}
      startupProbe: bounded-cold-start
      readinessProbe: traffic-contract
      livenessProbe: process-deadlock-only
      fault-test: throttling-and-oom
    verify: kubectl get pod status-api -o json && kubectl top pod status-api --containers
    failure: Reject dependency-based liveness, requests as runtime caps, green idle probes as capacity proof, or unexplained OOM and eviction.
    repair: Measure workload demand, separate probe purposes, calculate QoS, inject startup and pressure faults, and correlate container and node evidence.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
